start.text: Good Morning Uncle Lim! Sorry for disturbing you! You just took your BP meds. I'll have to check on your blood pressure for you!
start.speaker: player

options.response: Have at it! Don't worry dear! You're not a bother at all! I'm always glad to see you.

cuff_wrap.text: He beams at you while stretching his arm out obediently you gently spread the velcro apart and wrap the blood pressure cuff around his arm.
cuff_wrap.speaker: empty

relax_arm.text: Just relax your arm for me, Uncle. Keep it still... okay, perfect.
relax_arm.speaker: player

all_good.text: All good, Uncle! All prepared for your upcoming surgery!
all_good.speaker: player

notice_feet.text: You smile at him and begin packing up the equipment when you catch a glance of his mildly swollen feet. Your eyes can't help but gaze at it sadly as you place the blood pressure monitor back into its box.
notice_feet.speaker: empty

uncle_grabs_hand.text:
Uncle lim grabs your hand and squeezes it gently

Come on, don't be too sad over it! I'm not a pity case! I've lived with this for... three years already, you know? These 'water-balloon' legs may look like water balloons but they've carried me to dialysis and back, three times a week, over a thousand days... and they haven't failed me yet.
uncle_grabs_hand.speaker: character

pat_legs.text: Uncle Lim pats tenderly at his legs.
pat_legs.speaker: empty

blame_self.text:
Anyways I only have myself to blame.

You know, Nurse... people look at me and see a 'sick old man.' But when I was your age, I worked twelve-hour shifts at the Jurong Shipyard. Rain or shine, I was there. My kids needed school books, a roof... they needed a future.
blame_self.speaker: character

options.response: you worked hard for them uncle and im sure they appreciate it

coke_story.text: Every afternoon, when the Singapore sun was so hot and my whole shirt was drenched... I would grab a cold can of Coke. Sometimes two. That sugar hit was the only thing that kept me going until the night shift. My wife... aiyo, she would nag at me non-stop. 'Drink more plain water!'
coke_story.speaker: character

wife_nag.text: I used to laugh and tell her, 'Aiyo, you worry too much, one a day is fine!'
wife_nag.speaker: character

promise_me.text: Uncle, don't be too hard on yourself. But promise me one thing, okay?
promise_me.speaker: player

take_care.text: Once this transplant is over and you have that new kidney... you must remember to take care of yourself. No more 'one a day is fine' logic. You've done your part for the kids; now you have to stay healthy so you can actually enjoy being their father.
take_care.speaker: player

options.response:
(He looks at you, his eyes soft and wistful)

Aiyo, Nurse... you sound just like her. Exactly like my wife. She used to point her finger at me just like that whenever I reached for the fridge. I guess I finally have to admit she was the smart one.

kopi_kosong.text:
you grin cheekily

Next time, I want to see you drinking kopi o kosong in your hand, okay?
kopi_kosong.speaker: player

options.response: Okay, okay! I promise! This time, not even kopi o kosong, I will drink plain water! Hahaha!
